req.title: Bulk import employees
req.statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-097 requires bulk CSV import with per-row validation, partial commit, and structured error reporting. Test coverage is designed across four dimensions: (1) EP – a well-formed CSV with mixed valid/invalid rows exercises the core happy-path partition and partial-commit behaviour; (2) NEG – uploading a non-CSV file triggers the 415 error pathway; (3) NEG/DT – a CSV whose header omits required columns triggers the 422 error pathway; (4) BVA – a single-data-row CSV (minimum non-empty payload) probes the lower boundary of importable content; (5) STC – the state transition from 'no employees' through 'import initiated' to 'employees committed + error report available' is exercised by the positive case. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up state, and idempotent because teardown deletes any imported records by the seeded external-id.

tests.name: test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_commits_valid_rows_and_reports_errors
preconditions: Session cookie for hr-admin is available via <hr_admin_session> fixture
No employee with external_id EXT-001 or EXT-002 exists in the system
actions: Build a CSV with two valid rows (EXT-001, EXT-002) and one invalid row (missing required email field)
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import with the CSV file
expected: Response body contains errors list with exactly 1 entry referencing row 3
trace: R-WEB-097: commit only rows that pass validation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

CSV_MIXED = (
    "external_id,first_name,last_name,email\n"
    "EXT-001,Alice,Smith,alice@example.com\n"
    "EXT-002,Bob,Jones,bob@example.com\n"
    "EXT-BAD,Charlie,,\n"
)

def test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_commits_valid_rows_and_reports_errors(
    hr_admin_session,  # <fixture>
    api_base_url,      # <fixture>
    cleanup_employees, # <fixture> deletes by external_id on teardown
):
    cleanup_employees(["EXT-001", "EXT-002", "EXT-BAD"])
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.StringIO(CSV_MIXED), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 2
    assert len(body["errors"]) == 1
    assert body["errors"][0]["row"] == 3
    r1 = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees", params={"external_id": "EXT-001"})
    assert r1.status_code == 200 and r1.json()["total"] >= 1
    r2 = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees", params={"external_id": "EXT-002"})
    assert r2.status_code == 200 and r2.json()["total"] >= 1

tests.name: test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415
preconditions: Session cookie for hr-admin is available via <hr_admin_session> fixture
actions: Build a plain-text file with .txt extension and text/plain MIME type
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import with the text file
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing unsupported file type
trace: R-WEB-097: error pathway – 415 when file not CSV
tests.executable:
import io

def test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415(
    hr_admin_session,  # <fixture>
    api_base_url,      # <fixture>
):
    content = b"not,a,csv,file\nfoo,bar,baz"
    files = {"file": ("employees.txt", io.BytesIO(content), "text/plain")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 415
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    error_text = (body.get("error") or body.get("message", "")).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ["unsupported", "media type", "csv", "file type"])

tests.name: test_bulk_import_csv_missing_required_header_returns_422
preconditions: Session cookie for hr-admin is available via <hr_admin_session> fixture
actions: Build a CSV that omits the required 'email' column from its header row
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import with that CSV file
expected: Response body contains an error referencing the missing required column
trace: R-WEB-097: error pathway – 422 when header missing required columns
tests.executable:
import io

CSV_BAD_HEADER = (
    "external_id,first_name,last_name\n"
    "EXT-999,Dave,Brown\n"
)

def test_bulk_import_csv_missing_required_header_returns_422(
    hr_admin_session,  # <fixture>
    api_base_url,      # <fixture>
):
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.StringIO(CSV_BAD_HEADER), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body or "message" in body
    raw = str(body).lower()
    assert "email" in raw or "column" in raw or "header" in raw

tests.name: test_bulk_import_single_valid_row_csv_boundary
preconditions: Session cookie for hr-admin is available via <hr_admin_session> fixture
No employee with external_id EXT-BVA exists in the system
actions: Build a CSV with exactly one data row (minimum non-empty payload, BVA lower bound)
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import with the CSV file
Verify the single row is committed and error list is empty
expected: Response body contains an empty errors list
trace: R-WEB-097: boundary – minimum importable payload (1 data row)
tests.executable:
import io

CSV_SINGLE = (
    "external_id,first_name,last_name,email\n"
    "EXT-BVA,Eve,Taylor,eve@example.com\n"
)

def test_bulk_import_single_valid_row_csv_boundary(
    hr_admin_session,  # <fixture>
    api_base_url,      # <fixture>
    cleanup_employees, # <fixture>
):
    cleanup_employees(["EXT-BVA"])
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.StringIO(CSV_SINGLE), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 1
    assert body.get("errors", []) == []
    r = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees", params={"external_id": "EXT-BVA"})
    assert r.status_code == 200 and r.json()["total"] >= 1

tests.name: test_bulk_import_state_transition_no_employees_to_partial_import
preconditions: Session cookie for hr-admin is available via <hr_admin_session> fixture
System is in state: no employees with external_ids EXT-STC-A or EXT-STC-B exist
Import endpoint is in idle state (no in-flight import jobs)
actions: Transition: POST CSV with one valid row (EXT-STC-A) and one invalid row to /employees/import
Assert post-state: imported_count==1, errors list has 1 entry
Assert committed state: GET /employees?external_id=EXT-STC-A returns total==1
Assert rejected state: GET /employees?external_id=EXT-STC-B returns total==0
expected: Pre-state: no employees with tested external_ids
Transition returns 200/207 with imported_count==1 and 1 error
Post-state: EXT-STC-A employee exists; EXT-STC-B employee does not exist
trace: R-WEB-097: state transition – idle -> importing -> committed+error_reported
R-WEB-097: commit only rows that pass validation
tests.executable:
import io

CSV_STC = (
    "external_id,first_name,last_name,email\n"
    "EXT-STC-A,Frank,Moore,frank@example.com\n"
    "EXT-STC-B,,,\n"
)

def test_bulk_import_state_transition_no_employees_to_partial_import(
    hr_admin_session,  # <fixture>
    api_base_url,      # <fixture>
    cleanup_employees, # <fixture>
):
    cleanup_employees(["EXT-STC-A", "EXT-STC-B"])
    pre_a = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees", params={"external_id": "EXT-STC-A"})
    assert pre_a.json()["total"] == 0, "Pre-state violated: EXT-STC-A already exists"
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.StringIO(CSV_STC), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 1
    assert len(body["errors"]) == 1
    post_a = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees", params={"external_id": "EXT-STC-A"})
    assert post_a.json()["total"] == 1
    post_b = hr_admin_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/employees", params={"external_id": "EXT-STC-B"})
    assert post_b.json()["total"] == 0
